top10.introHtml: Ranked for a cheap, fun second car — condition and reliability first, mileage second. Miatas and Solstice GXPs dominate the value tier; a low-mile classic Mustang GT and a BMW 325Ci with surprisingly low miles round out the picks.

picks.whyGood: Newest Miata generation (ND platform, 2016+) available in budget — the gold standard for cheap fun convertibles. Only 61,200 miles, Great Deal rating, and the ND is the most refined Miata ever built. Timing chain, no belt to worry about. Will depreciate slowly and parts are plentiful.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through a full open/close cycle — check latch, rear window clarity, and headliner for water staining. Inspect rocker panels and floor drains (Miata traps leaves). Verify timing chain tensioner is quiet at cold start. Check CarFax for any flood or salvage flag.
picks.bottomLine: Best modern reliable fun-per-dollar convertible in the search — buy this if it checks out on PPI.

picks.whyGood: Only 37,018 miles on a 17-year-old Solstice — exceptional low mileage for this platform. Nine miles away, Great Deal rating, and the Kappa chassis is genuinely fun with near-50/50 balance. The base 2.4L is reliable and parts are still available from GM.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through full cycle; check rear window seam for delamination and headliner for water stains. Inspect rocker panels for rust (Kappa convertibles can trap moisture). Check for GM 2.4L oil consumption. Verify top motor operates smoothly — replacements are pricey.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile Solstice this close and this cheap is rare — top PPI priority.

picks.whyGood: GXP adds a 260-hp turbocharged 2.0L and Bilstein shocks — this is the hot version. $8,195 for a turbo convertible with clean title and no accidents is excellent value. 88k miles is reasonable for a turbo Kappa. 18 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: Check boost system: inspect intercooler connections, check for boost leaks, verify the turbo spools cleanly without smoke or lag. Top operation and rear window seam critical. Rocker panel rust inspection. Oil consumption test on turbo engine. Ask for any service records on the turbo.
picks.bottomLine: A turbo Solstice for $8k with clean title is genuinely rare — strong value pick if the boost system checks out.

picks.whyGood: NC-generation Miata (2006–2015) — heavier than the classic NB but more refined, larger engine (2.0L), and exceptionally reliable. 82k miles is nothing for this platform; NC Miatas routinely run past 200k. Timing chain. $10,995 at a reputable dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation (power soft-top on NC): listen for hydraulic pump noise, check for slow/jerky operation indicating low fluid or worn pump. Check rear window clarity. Inspect for rust under floor mats — NC traps water in door drains. Verify no oil leaks from rear main seal.
picks.bottomLine: Reliable, proven platform with decades of parts support — solid second-choice Miata if the Club above is gone.

picks.whyGood: BMW E46 325Ci convertible with only 60,200 miles — low for a 24-year-old car. The M54 inline-6 is one of BMW's great naturally-aspirated engines. 15 miles away, clean title, no accidents. At $10k this is priced right for the mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: BMW E46 soft-top hydraulic system: operate roof fully, listen for pump strain. Check for rust behind the rear seat (common E46 convertible moisture intrusion). Inspect coolant expansion tank (brittle, often cracked on E46). Oil leaks from valve cover and VANOS. Check subframe reinforcement — E46 subframe cracking is a known issue.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile E46 at a fair price — worth a PPI with an independent BMW specialist before buying.

picks.whyGood: Only 44,000 miles on a 1994 SN-95 Mustang GT convertible — that's remarkable. The 5.0L V8 is a proven, simple engine with a huge aftermarket. Private seller (less markup, more history available). Clean title, no accidents reported.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 Mustang convertible frame: inspect top mount points where the convertible reinforcement meets the rocker panel — rust here is structural. Operate the soft top fully. Check for leaks around rear window. Verify originality given the low mileage (odometer rollback is rare but possible on a 30-year-old private sale). T-5 transmission third-gear synchro test. Rear axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: Rare low-mile SN-95 V8 convertible from a private seller — verify the miles and get a structural inspection before buying.

picks.whyGood: 2007 GXP turbo with only 51,730 miles — better mileage than the Pennsauken GXP above, though $3,800 more. The Solstice GXP with a manual is one of the most entertaining cheap convertibles ever built. 25 miles away at a dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: Same GXP turbo checklist: boost system health, intercooler condition, turbo smoke/spool test. Top motor operation. Rocker rust. Ask dealer if the transmission is manual or automatic — manual strongly preferred for the GXP experience. Check for any oil consumption on the LNF turbo engine.
picks.bottomLine: Higher-mileage alternative GXP for $12k — worth it if the Pennsauken one falls through.

picks.whyGood: An astonishing 29,900 miles on a 15-year-old MINI Cooper S convertible. The R57 Cooper S (turbocharged 1.6T) is fun and the low mileage suggests a pampered garage car. Clean title, no accidents. At $11,895 this is fairly priced for the mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: MINI R57 soft-top: operate fully, check for tears or yellowed rear window. N18 turbo engine: check for oil leaks at valve cover and timing chain tensioner noise (cold start rattle is a known R56/R57 issue). Inspect coolant expansion tank. Electric soft-top motor reliability — test multiple cycles. Ask about service history given unusually low miles.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage car in the entire search — condition likely excellent, but MINI reliability requires a specialist PPI.

picks.whyGood: Classic Z3 roadster with the 2.3L inline-6 — smooth, rev-happy, and genuinely pretty. 75,828 miles is appropriate for the age. Clean title, no accidents, at Selden Motors (a local specialist that appears multiple times in this search). $9,990.
picks.whatToVerify: BMW Z3 soft-top: check for tears, zipper condition, and rear window delamination (very common). Inspect coolant expansion tank and cooling system — E36 era components are brittle. Oil leaks from valve cover gasket and rear main seal. Check for rust in the footwell (Z3 is known for water leaks into cabin). Verify manual transmission (preferred).
picks.bottomLine: Classic roadster at a reasonable price — a good choice for weekend fun with manageable maintenance costs.

picks.whyGood: Best value in the entire search at $7,500 with a Great Deal rating. 62,995 miles is reasonable. The base Solstice is still a genuinely fun rear-drive roadster — sharp steering, good balance, and the 2.4L is durable. Budget pick that leaves $7,500 in your pocket for maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation and rear window seam (same Kappa platform as other Solstices above). Rocker panel rust inspection. GM 2.4L ECOTEC oil consumption. Top motor and latch mechanism — check for smoothness. Verify no structural rust given the price.
picks.bottomLine: The budget pick — $7,500 for a rear-drive roadster with a Great Deal rating is hard to beat if the top and body check out.

mustangs.introHtml: Three Ford Mustang convertibles found within 35 miles. Two SN-95 V8 GTs (1994–1995) with low miles lead the pack; a 2012 S197 V6 close to home offers the most modern driving experience.

picks.whyGood: The lowest-mileage Mustang convertible in the search at just 44,000 miles on the SN-95 GT's 5.0L V8 — a simple, bulletproof pushrod engine. Private seller means more history and less markup. Clean title, no accidents.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible frame: inspect top-mount-to-rocker junction for rust (structural). Full top operation. T-5 third-gear synchro (slow-shift to 3rd cold). 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff. Verify mileage plausibility with records — a 30-year-old private sale at 44k deserves scrutiny.
picks.bottomLine: Rare low-mile V8 SN-95 drop-top — frame inspection is the make-or-break item.

picks.whyGood: Another SN-95 GT with reasonable 55,630 miles. The 1995 GT still has the classic 5.0L in its final year before the 4.6L switch — desirable to Mustang enthusiasts and simpler to work on. 25 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checklist: frame rust at top mount, full top operation, T-5 synchro test, 8.8 axle clunk. Water staining on rear seats from top seal failure. Check for any signs of modifications that might mask issues.
picks.bottomLine: Solid SN-95 alternative — the final 5.0L year adds a small collector appeal.

picks.whyGood: S197 generation Mustang (2005–2014) — much stiffer chassis and better handling than the SN-95. The 3.7L V6 makes 305hp and gets better fuel economy than the V8. 9 miles away is very convenient. Fair Deal at $11,459.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible top: operate through full cycle, check for rear window sealing (water stains on rear seat). IRS-equipped cars (rare on V6 S197): check clunk on takeoff. T-6060 six-speed synchro test if manual. Check for rust at the convertible top hydraulic lines. Inspect for signs of track use or modifications.
picks.bottomLine: The most modern Mustang drop-top in budget — closest to home and the easiest daily driver of the three.
